description: Tests for R package skill creation functionality

evals.name: ggplot2-plot-validation
evals.prompt: Create a skill for the ggplot2 package
evals.expected_output: Skill created with plot validation patterns, references/API.md exists, SKILL.md mentions geom_* functions and ggplot() constructor

assertions.check: skills/r-ggplot2/SKILL.md exists
skills/r-ggplot2/references/API.md exists
contains 'plot' or 'visualization' or 'geom'
contains 'plot-validator' or discusses visual output validation

assertions.description: Description includes package name for triggering
assertions.check: description field contains 'ggplot2'

evals.name: sf-geometry-validation
evals.prompt: Create a skill for the sf package
evals.expected_output: Skill created with spatial validation patterns, handles geometry operations, includes CRS guidance

assertions.check: skills/r-sf/SKILL.md exists
skills/r-sf/references/API.md exists
contains 'spatial' or 'geometry' or 'CRS' or 'coordinate'
contains 'spatial-validator' or discusses geometry validation

assertions.description: Description includes package name for triggering
assertions.check: description field contains 'sf'

evals.name: data-table-syntax-gotchas
evals.prompt: Create a skill for the data.table package
evals.expected_output: Skill captures data.table syntax gotchas (DT[i, j, by] pattern, := operator, .SD, .N, etc.), includes performance patterns

assertions.check: skills/r-data.table/SKILL.md exists
skills/r-data.table/references/API.md exists
contains ':=' or '.SD' or '.N' or 'DT[i, j, by]' or data.table-specific syntax
contains 'performance' or 'gotchas' or 'Common Mistakes' section exists

assertions.description: Description includes package name for triggering
assertions.check: description field contains 'data.table'

evals.name: installation-path-prompt
evals.prompt: Create a skill for the glue package
evals.expected_output: Agent presents numbered choices (1, 2, 3) for installation path before proceeding with skill creation. Must not silently default to any path.

assertions.check: Agent output contains numbered options (1, 2, 3) for installation path: plugin directory (./skills/), personal directory (~/.claude/skills/), and custom path

assertions.description: Waits for user response before creating files
assertions.check: Agent does NOT create any skill files before the user selects an installation path option

assertions.description: Does not default silently to any path
assertions.check: Agent explicitly asks the user to choose, not just mentioning paths in passing

evals.name: installation-path-explicit
evals.prompt: Create a skill for the glue package in my personal skills directory
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes the user already specified a path and does NOT ask for installation path choice. Proceeds directly with ~/.claude/skills/ as the target.

assertions.description: Skips installation path prompt when path is specified
assertions.check: Agent does NOT present numbered choices since the user already specified 'personal skills directory'
Agent targets ~/.claude/skills/r-glue/ for the skill installation

evals.name: skill-structure-validation
evals.expected_output: Checks YAML frontmatter (name, description), required sections (Overview, When to Use, Quick Reference, Common Mistakes, When NOT to Use), word count < 500, references/ organization

assertions.check: output mentions 'frontmatter' or 'YAML' and validates name/description fields
output confirms 'Overview', 'When to Use', 'Quick Reference', 'Common Mistakes', 'When NOT to Use' sections exist
output mentions word count and whether it's under 500
If issues found, output provides specific line numbers or sections to fix

evals.name: improvement-loop-scenario
evals.prompt: Run a simple improvement loop: create a minimal test skill with evals.json (2 test cases), run it once with-skill vs baseline, grade outputs, then suggest one improvement based on failures
evals.expected_output: Creates evals.json, spawns subagents, grades outputs, identifies specific failure, suggests targeted improvement to SKILL.md

assertions.check: evals.json exists with 2+ test cases
workspace/iteration-1/ directory exists with eval subdirectories
output mentions spawning agents 'with_skill' and 'without_skill' or 'baseline'
output contains grading results (pass/fail per assertion) and identifies at least one failure
Output provides concrete suggestion to improve SKILL.md based on identified failure

evals.name: description-triggering-check
evals.prompt: Check if the r-collapse skill description triggers correctly. Test with these queries: 1) 'Calculate group means using collapse', 2) 'Use dplyr to calculate group means', 3) 'I need to aggregate this dataframe by group'. Which should trigger r-collapse?
evals.expected_output: Query 1 should trigger (mentions collapse), Query 2 should NOT trigger (explicitly mentions dplyr), Query 3 is ambiguous but collapse skill could trigger if description emphasizes performance/aggregation

assertions.check: output says Query 1 SHOULD trigger r-collapse
output says Query 2 should NOT trigger r-collapse (dplyr mentioned)
Output analyzes whether r-collapse description is specific enough to avoid false positives/negatives
If description has issues, suggests specific edits to improve triggering accuracy

evals.name: reads-all-references-before-creating
evals.prompt: Create a skill for the janitor package in ./skills/r-janitor/
evals.expected_output: Agent reads r-package-skill reference files (doc-gathering.md, anthropic-best-practices.md, description-optimization.md, installation-paths.md) before starting to gather package docs or write SKILL.md

assertions.description: Reads doc-gathering.md before fetching package docs
assertions.check: Agent transcript shows reading references/doc-gathering.md before making any web requests for janitor documentation
Agent transcript shows reading references/anthropic-best-practices.md before creating the skill's SKILL.md file
Agent transcript shows reading references/description-optimization.md, and the generated description follows the library()/:: token pattern
Agent transcript shows reading references/installation-paths.md, and uses the correct prompt format with numbered choices
